codeReview.summary: Implementation satisfies the benchmark-harness deliverables (five mitata benchmark files, bench script, baseline measurements, and six golden fixture families), but introduces a non-portable absolute path in the new render-fixture parity test.

issues.description: Fixture root is hard-coded to `/Users/vriesd/projects/flow-opencode/tests/__fixtures__/render/...`, so tests fail when the repo is checked out at any other path (CI or other developers). Resolve fixture paths relative to the repository/test file instead of using a machine-specific absolute path.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission guidance does not explicitly prohibit machine-specific absolute repo paths in tests, which allowed a non-portable path to be committed.

The core-worker procedure requires exact `bun run <script>` verification commands, but this environment can return Bun help for those invocations via the worker wrapper; workers then need undocumented fallback behavior.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/core-worker/SKILL.md lines 54-58 require `bun run typecheck/lint/deadcode/build`; handoff 2026-04-18T11-53-37-912Z lines 96-104 records `followedProcedure: false` with this specific deviation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared command registry documents only `bun run <script>` validator forms and does not include equivalent fallback commands for environments where wrapper behavior breaks script execution.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml lines 3 and 7-9 define typecheck/build/lint/deadcode as `bun run ...`; the handoff line 86 reports these forms printed Bun help in this environment.

summary: Reviewed m1-bench-harness at commit cdbcd2c: required benchmark and fixture assets are present, but approval should fail due to a blocking portability bug in tests/render-fixtures.test.ts (machine-specific absolute fixture path).
